attribution_discipline.declared_original_claim: Verbatim or faithful paraphrase from candidate-classes.json, targets.json, scenario catalog, or committed evidence
attribution_discipline.declared_assumption_or_dependency: Platform dependency or boundary declared but not claimed as implemented
attribution_discipline.reviewer_interpretation: Reviewer conclusion derived from code-path inspection; not attributed as historical declaration

findings.declared_original_claim: Append-only JSONL canonical log plus generation manifest and optional checkpoint snapshots (candidate-classes.json authoritative_state_model)
findings.reviewer_interpretation: Append-oriented authoritative log with replay recovery
findings.observed_implementation: AppendRecord stores seq/command_kind/generation only; replay_log validates sequence and returns checkpoint state unchanged
findings.required_action: Implement command payload in log and deterministic replay, or reclassify candidate as checkpoint-snapshot bundle

findings.declared_original_claim: Optional generation rewrite compacts log into new checkpoint generation (candidate-classes.json compaction_gc_behavior)
findings.reviewer_interpretation: Compaction preserves valid recovery path after interrupt
findings.observed_implementation: Compaction truncates log and bumps generation without resetting committed_seq; replay_log expects seq from 1; interrupted-compaction fault fires before_compaction_mutation with no post-fault observation
findings.required_action: Fix seq/generation contract after compaction; add post-interrupt reopen validation in separate scenario

findings.declared_original_claim: Dedicated writer_lock table row / writer.lock token file with second-writer rejection (candidate-classes.json single_writer_strategy)
findings.declared_assumption_or_dependency: Append candidate: stale ownership requires explicit takeover with integrity reload (declared strategy, not demonstrated)
findings.reviewer_interpretation: Single-writer enforcement with crash recovery and takeover
findings.observed_implementation: Writer token stored in DB row or manifest; cleared only on close; no takeover or abandoned-writer recovery
findings.unsupported_higher_claim: ProcessCrashRecovery single-writer correctness; MD-015 crash-held-writer experiments
findings.required_action: Add stale-writer recovery with integrity validation; do not rely on PID absence

findings.declared_original_claim: New session directory and database copied under new session identity with duplicated_from lineage metadata (candidate-classes.json duplication_behavior)
findings.declared_assumption_or_dependency: Filesystem copy of session.db tested only as spike control; not a product backup feature (backup_boundary)
findings.reviewer_interpretation: The implemented session.db-only copy is not WAL-safe under enabled journal_mode=WAL
findings.observed_implementation: PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL enabled; duplicate_session uses fs::copy of session.db only without -wal/-shm or backup API
findings.required_action: Use SQLite backup API or checkpoint-and-copy protocol; test duplication while source writer is open

findings.declared_original_claim: Candidate A delegates durability/recovery partly to embedded transactional engine with relational constraints; Candidate B uses append-dominant authoritative log, explicit manifest generations, and replay/checkpoint recovery (material_distinctness_summary)
findings.reviewer_interpretation: The originally declared authority-model distinction — relational canonical entity authority versus append-authoritative replay — is not demonstrated by the current implementations
findings.observed_implementation: Both candidates persist full normalized-state JSON snapshots as effective canonical representation (SQLite state_json row vs checkpoint file); append log is non-authoritative stub
findings.unsupported_higher_claim: Declared authority-model distinctness for mechanism comparison readiness
findings.required_action: Rewrite candidate class declarations to match actual authority models or rebuild mechanisms; do not conflate with total mechanism identity

findings.declared_original_claim: Authoritative command returns Ok only after append record fsync and manifest generation bump via atomic rename (candidate-classes.json durable_acknowledgement_boundary)
findings.reviewer_interpretation: Observed implementation does not perform checkpoint, manifest-temp, or parent-directory fsync
findings.observed_implementation: Only append log path calls sync_all; checkpoint and manifest use temp write + rename without file or directory fsync
findings.unsupported_higher_claim: FilesystemDurability acknowledgement boundary; directory fsync as implemented behavior
findings.required_action: Add durable sync for checkpoint, manifest, and parent directory where supported

findings.declared_original_claim: Canonical entities stored relationally with explicit BEGIN/COMMIT boundaries (candidate-classes.json authoritative_state_model); SQLite integrity check plus semantic reload validation (corruption_behavior)
findings.reviewer_interpretation: Relational canonical entity storage with integrity check
findings.observed_implementation: Canonical state is single state_json blob; no PRAGMA integrity_check; tables are meta/lock/derived shell
findings.unsupported_higher_claim: Embedded transactional database with relational authority
findings.required_action: Reclassify as SQLite-hosted JSON snapshot or implement relational decomposition

findings.declared_original_claim: Classify and rebuild derived-state corruption (scenario catalog description; targets.json pass_requires derived corruption rebuildable without canonical loss)
findings.observed_implementation: Derived artifacts corrupted but open/read paths load canonical only; derived never consulted or rebuilt; oracle compares fixture to actual
findings.unsupported_higher_claim: Derived corruption classification and rebuild semantics
findings.required_action: Assert derived detect/rebuild behavior on reopen or downgrade scenario claim

findings.declared_original_claim: Create a new session identity with source lineage (scenario catalog); duplication creates independent session identity (targets.json pass_requires)
findings.reviewer_interpretation: Duplication independence and oracle-valid copied history
findings.observed_implementation: Oracle compares duplicate.normalized_state to itself; no persisted reopen of source or duplicate; adapter-return-only check
findings.required_action: Reopen source and duplicate from disk; compare against independent expected state; verify source unchanged

findings.declared_original_claim: Interrupt an authoritative transition around acknowledgement (scenario catalog; FailureModel::ProcessInterruption)
findings.declared_assumption_or_dependency: targets.json pass_requires interrupted transition does not expose partial authoritative state as valid
findings.observed_implementation: FailBeforeDurabilityCommit fires before any write/transaction; reopen proves unchanged state trivially
findings.required_action: Add mid-write and post-ack fault points in separate scenarios; classify current handler as logical pre-commit abort

findings.reviewer_interpretation: Interrupted compaction does not expose mixed canonical state
findings.observed_implementation: Fault fires before_compaction_mutation; pass on simulated-compaction-interrupt error with fixture-to-fixture oracle; no compaction write occurred; no reopen or post-fault state validation
findings.required_action: Reopen after in-progress compaction interrupt in separate scenario; invalidate current Passed as recovery evidence

findings.declared_original_claim: Permit at most one authoritative writer (scenario catalog); at most one authoritative writer (targets.json pass_requires)
findings.observed_implementation: Second open rejected with writer-already-open; oracle self-compares before to before; no crash/orphan lock recovery
findings.required_action: Replace self-compare; add orphan-lock and crash-held-writer recovery as separate scenarios

findings.declared_original_claim: Semantic oracle pass for semantic correctness scenarios (targets.json pass_requires semantic oracle pass)
findings.observed_implementation: passed_oracle(fixture, fixture) tautology in multiple handlers; canonical corruption accepts any Err
findings.unsupported_higher_claim: SemanticCorrectness oracle validation at LogicalStateTransition or above
findings.required_action: Use independent expected observation or non-oracle pass criteria; require specific error codes

findings.declared_assumption_or_dependency: Eligibility note: host-platform correctness eligibility only; does not select a mechanism
findings.observed_implementation: 13 Passed scenarios include tautological oracle passes and logical-only faults; class declarations exceed implementation
findings.required_action: Downgrade eligibility to catalog-pass only; selection_status remains none; fail-closed invariant applies

findings.declared_original_claim: Durable acknowledgement only after commit boundary (targets.json pass_requires)
findings.reviewer_interpretation: Durable authoritative command persistence including ProcessCrashRecovery
findings.observed_implementation: Same-handle read after apply; no close-to-reopen cycle; expected built with shared apply_command
findings.required_action: Add close-to-reopen after every persistence claim; separate expected oracle from adapter apply path

findings.declared_original_claim: Reject stale commands without mutation (targets.json pass_requires stale commands rejected without mutation)
findings.observed_implementation: Single-handle stale precondition rejection with before/after compare; no multi-actor concurrency
findings.required_action: Reclassify failure_model to scoped-precondition; retain as valid lower-strength evidence

findings.declared_original_claim: Interrupt candidate-supported destructive historical cleanup (scenario catalog; capability-dependent)
findings.declared_assumption_or_dependency: Destructive historical GC not implemented in spike (candidate-classes.json known_limitations)
findings.observed_implementation: Unsupported due to missing capability; correctly retained as negative/unsupported
findings.required_action: Retain Unsupported status; deferred until destructive GC capability exists and is separately authorized

findings.declared_original_claim: Fault injection is logical-layer only; not hardware power-loss equivalence (candidate-classes.json known_limitations)
findings.declared_assumption_or_dependency: No hardware power-loss injection; No OS-level crash injection in spike
findings.observed_implementation: FaultLayer::Logical only; limitations.json records hardware_power_loss not tested; no fsync-at-fault injection
findings.required_action: Add filesystem-layer fault injection and platform evidence in separately authorized packages
